item.title: New Directors, New Films
item.description: Also: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson in “The Drama,” Michael Schulman on spring fabulosity, Rachel Syme on the latest in trenchcoats, and more.

item.title: Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth’s Warped Vision of the Iran War
item.description: The two men might wish that they lived in a world where whoever dropped the most bombs got whatever he wanted. But the war has shown that this isn’t true.
item.dc:creator: ByBenjamin Wallace-Wells

item.title: Geese, Cooked
item.description: As bird flu hits the Hamptons, Long Island’s fanciest beaches are becoming mass graves for felled fowl.
item.dc:creator: ByEmma Allen

item.title: Happy Hour with Emanuel Ax
item.description: To ring in his new WQXR podcast, the veteran pianist puts on a special live show with a secret surprise guest—his old drinking buddy Yo-Yo Ma.
item.dc:creator: ByJane Bua

item.title: Getting Older with Clare Barron and Anne Kauffman
item.description: At the Cherry Lane Theatre, the writer and the director of “You Got Older,” starring Alia Shawkat and Peter Friedman, dish on mortality, romantic angst, and the rapper Pitbull.
item.dc:creator: ByAlex Barasch

item.title: Do the Circulation-Desk Shuffle
item.description: The New York Public Library’s new series, Lunch Dances, features choreography based on objects in the stacks. Can a pirouette tell the story of a mid-century lesbian magazine?
item.dc:creator: ByNaaman Zhou

item.title: Why Are People Injecting Themselves with Peptides?
item.description: Health and wellness influencers are hawking unapproved treatments on the gray market. The future of the F.D.A.—and the health of consumers—is at stake.
item.dc:creator: ByDhruv Khullar

item.title: Can Sponge Cities Save Us from the Coming Floods?
item.description: As the planet gets warmer and the rains fall harder, the future of flood control is looking less like a wall and something more like a park.
item.dc:creator: ByEric Klinenberg

item.title: Sam Altman May Control Our Future—Can He Be Trusted?
item.description: New interviews and closely guarded documents shed light on the persistent doubts about the head of OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: ByRonan FarrowandAndrew Marantz

item.title: How the Internet Fringe Infiltrated Republican Politics
item.description: Inside the battle for the post-MAGAG.O.P.
item.dc:creator: ByAntonia Hitchens

item.title: Our Mom-and-Pop Data Center
item.description: Mornin’, tech brothers and sisters! Come take a walk on our information super country road.
item.dc:creator: ByJed FeimanandNehemiah Markos

item.title: “Rate Your Happiness”
item.description: How natural it is to fail, to fail to decide, to remain in meaningless motion.
item.dc:creator: ByCatherine Lacey

item.title: We Are All Constantly Mutating—and That’s a Good Thing
item.description: Genetic research has been complicating the idea of the genome as a determinative blueprint.
item.dc:creator: ByJerome Groopman

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “In the Days of My Youth I Was Told What It Means to Be a Man,” “True Color,” “Half His Age,” and “Under Water.”

item.title: Will Biblical Womanhood Box You In or Set You Free?
item.description: Two writers of different evangelical generations offer rival visions of marriage, motherhood, and ambition.
item.dc:creator: ByEmma Green

item.title: In Marie NDiaye’s Spellbinding New Novel, Witchcraft Stays in the Family
item.description: In “The Witch,” a mother passes to her daughters a secret, burdensome power, but sorcery can’t fix a household that’s coming apart.
item.dc:creator: ByKristen Roupenian

item.title: The New Museum Returns, but Humans Are Left Behind
item.description: After an eighty-two-million-dollar renovation, the museum has put on a sprawling show about the war between humanity and technology. We seem to be losing.
item.dc:creator: ByZachary Fine

item.title: Kanye West Makes a Record for the A.I. Era
item.description: Fans want to know whether the vocals on his new album, “Bully,” are truly his. But the question of what the “real” Ye sounds like has never been simple.
item.dc:creator: ByKelefa Sanneh

item.title: Broadway’s “Dog Day Afternoon” Is a Dog
item.description: Sidney Lumet’s kinetic, emotionally complex film has been transformed into a hokey sitcom with gunshots.
item.dc:creator: ByEmily Nussbaum

item.title: Savannah Guthrie’s Excruciating Story, on “Today”
item.description: The morning-show host recounted the disappearance of her mother, Nancy, and its aftermath in boldly religious terms, as millions of viewers watched.
item.dc:creator: ByVinson Cunningham

item.title: An Artists’ Duel Proves Restorative in “The Christophers”
item.description: In Steven Soderbergh’s film, Ian McKellen and Michaela Coel are superbly matched as two skilled painters who find their way from slippery deception to common ground.
item.dc:creator: ByJustin Chang

item.title: “Theodore Roosevelt Taylor”
item.description: “In short, he slid metal on string till the devil / got tickled and laughed up the Blues.”
item.dc:creator: ByTyehimba Jess

item.title: “Meanwhile It Rains for Two Weeks and the Heat Never Breaks”
item.description: “Sometimes I text my friends I’m crying / and they replylol.”
item.dc:creator: ByMorgan Parker

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the April 13, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Monday, April 6, 2026
item.description: Today’s theme: Overdrive.
item.dc:creator: ByKareem Ayas
